rxn_class_summary: Electrochemically driven 1,2-halonitration (bromo/chloro-nitration) of alkenes using ferric nitrate as nitryl radical source and LiX as halide source
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of Fe(NO3)3·9H2O activates nitrate, generating nitryl radical (•NO2) and Fe(II); the injected electron is substoichiometric and acts catalytically (electron as catalyst)

key_intermediates.name: NO3 dianion radical (NO3•2-) from pulse radiolysis (λmax 350 nm)
Fe(II) species (regenerated, undergoes anodic reoxidation outside main cycle)

electron_flow: Cathode injects one electron into Fe(NO3)3·9H2O, producing Fe(II) and releasing •NO2 via SET reduction of nitrate (NO3- -> NO3•2- -> •NO2). •NO2 adds to the alkene to give a β-nitroalkyl C-radical, which undergoes inner-sphere radical ligand transfer from an in-situ formed Fe(III)-X intermediate to deliver the C-X bond and regenerate Fe(II); Fe(II) is reoxidized anodically, closing the catalytic cycle with substoichiometric electrons.
rate_determining_step: Single-electron reduction/activation of Fe(NO3)3·9H2O to release •NO2 (initiation); current-controlled, with on/off experiments showing slow continued conversion after current off

probe_reagents.name: Aryl cyclopropyl alkene (radical clock 3)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened bromo-nitro product 4 obtained in 87% (E:Z 1.1:1), confirming C-radical intermediate after •NO2 addition

probe_reagents.name: Allyl phenyl sulfone (5)
probe_reagents.observation: β-scission/desulfonylation product 6 (41%) consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (E)- and (Z)-stilbene
probe_reagents.observation: Both give product 7 with similar d.r. (10:3), indicating loss of olefin geometry via free C-radical

probe_reagents.name: Cesium nitrate / pulse radiolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Transient at λmax = 350 nm decaying by 2nd-order kinetics, assigned to NO3•2- from one-electron reduction of NO3-

probe_reagents.name: Substituted styrenes (Hammett series: tBu, H, F, Cl, Br, CN, NO2)
probe_reagents.observation: Slightly negative ρ ≈ -0.2, consistent with electrophilic •NO2 addition to alkene

probe_reagents.name: Nitrite (NO2-) detection (Griess-type test)
probe_reagents.observation: NO2- formed only when electricity is passed through Fe(NO3)3 solution, confirming electrochemical generation of •NO2

probe_reagents.name: Fe(OTf)2 / LiNO3 control (test F, I)
probe_reagents.observation: Mixing Fe(II) with nitrate also produces NO2-, supporting Fe(II) role in •NO2 regeneration

probe_reagents.name: On/off current experiments
probe_reagents.observation: Conversion continues during off periods; TON higher with longer off intervals, supporting substoichiometric electron (electrocatalysis) rather than stoichiometric radical chain initiation

notes: Authors frame the electron itself as a substoichiometric catalyst (electrocatalysis, scenario a) but also present the classical Fe(III)/Fe(II) redox cycle (scenario b) as equivalent representation. XPS of precipitate shows Fe(II)/Fe(III) oxide-hydroxide byproducts. Direct cathodic reduction of styrene (~ -1.2 V) is excluded since electrolysis at 0.77 V still works. An alternative inner-sphere pathway where the β-nitroalkyl radical is stabilized by Fe coordination prior to ligand transfer cannot be ruled out. Oxygen-atom transfer from NO3- to Fe(II) as an alternative to SET is also acknowledged.
